_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 8-8 Air Ambulance Personnel Scope (Revised 12/27/2022, MDHHS Reviewed 2022). Base Michigan template — MCA-specific selections not configured.
protocol_reference: Michigan System Protocol Section 8-8: Air Ambulance Personnel Scope — MDHHS Approved 12/27/2022
purpose: MCA OPTIONAL. Govern the scope of practice for air ambulance (helicopter and fixed-wing) medical personnel operating within a Michigan MCA's jurisdiction. Air medical personnel may operate under the sending MCA's protocols, the receiving MCA's protocols, or air medical service-specific protocols as defined by the MCA. MCAs must define which protocol set air medical personnel may operate under.

sections.title: Authorization and Protocol Use
sections.reference: Michigan Protocol 8-8: Air Ambulance Scope — Authorization
points: MCA OPTIONAL — MCAs may define the scope and protocol set for air ambulance personnel operating in their jurisdiction
Air medical personnel may operate under: MCA protocols applicable to their licensure level, or air medical service-specific protocols approved by the air medical service's medical director
Air medical personnel must be licensed at the appropriate Michigan EMS provider level OR meet equivalent licensure requirements
MCAs must clearly define whether air medical personnel are authorized to perform skills beyond those in the base Michigan protocols when operating in their jurisdiction

sections.title: Scope of Practice
sections.reference: Michigan Protocol 8-8: Air Ambulance Scope — Scope
points: Air medical personnel scope is limited to their Michigan EMS licensure level (or equivalent)
Critical Care Paramedics (CCP) and Enhanced Paramedics (EP) may operate at their credentialed level under MCA authorization
Skills not in the base Michigan protocols (e.g., blood products, vasoactive drips, RSI) require MCA authorization and air medical service medical director approval
Air medical personnel assuming care from ground EMS must conduct formal transfer of care per Section 8-5

sections.title: Transfer of Care — Air Medical
sections.reference: Michigan Protocol 8-8: Air Ambulance Scope — Transfer of Care
points: Transfer from ground EMS to air medical: ground crew provides verbal report; air medical crew acknowledges receipt
Transfer from air medical to receiving hospital: air medical crew provides report to accepting provider
Both parties document the transfer in their respective records
Landing zone (LZ) safety and patient packaging are responsibilities of the requesting ground crew per Section 8-9: Helicopter Utilization

mca_selections_required.description: MCA must define which protocol set air ambulance personnel may operate under in their jurisdiction
options: Air ambulance personnel operate under MCA protocols at their licensure level
Air ambulance personnel operate under air medical service-specific protocols approved by air medical medical director
Not authorized — no air ambulance operations in this MCA jurisdiction

mca_selections_required.description: MCA may authorize Enhanced Paramedic or Critical Care Paramedic air medical personnel to operate at their expanded scope
options: Authorized — EP/CCP scope permitted for credentialed air medical personnel
Not authorized — base Paramedic scope only

deterioration_flags: Air medical personnel request skills beyond MCA authorization — contact Medical Control; document deviation
Scope conflict between air medical and ground EMS protocols — air medical provider operates under their authorized protocol set; document any conflict
scope_notes: MCA OPTIONAL — MCA must define air ambulance scope and protocol set
Air medical personnel limited to Michigan EMS licensure level or equivalent
CCP/EP scope requires MCA authorization and air medical medical director approval
Skills beyond base Michigan protocols require MCA authorization
Transfer of care: formal verbal report required; both parties document
LZ safety: ground crew responsibility per Section 8-9
